name: Triage Agent Evaluations — 15 Real-World SOC Scenarios

parts.text:
## TASK

Triage the following Chronicle SecOps case following your instructions.

**Case ID:** CASE-EVAL-001
**Client ID:** eval-client
**Alert Type:** PHISHING
**Initial Severity:** HIGH
**Autonomous Mode:** True

Additional context:
User john.doe@company.com received a suspicious email with a link to login-secure.example.com. The user clicked the link and may have entered credentials. Source IP 203.0.113.42 is flagged in threat intel.

Based on my triage analysis of CASE-EVAL-001:

```json
{
  "case_id": "CASE-EVAL-001",
  "alert_type": "PHISHING",
  "verdict": "MALICIOUS",
  "priority": "HIGH",
  "confidence_score": 0.92,
  "recommended_action": "CONTAIN",
  "key_indicators": ["credential_harvesting_page", "malicious_ip_203.0.113.42", "user_credentials_submitted"],
  "iocs_found": [{"type": "IP", "value": "203.0.113.42"}, {"type": "DOMAIN", "value": "login-secure.example.com"}, {"type": "USER", "value": "john.doe@company.com"}],
  "summary": "High-confidence phishing attack. User john.doe clicked malicious link and submitted credentials to known phishing domain. IP 203.0.113.42 is confirmed malicious C2 infrastructure.",
  "escalation_notes": "Immediate credential reset required for john.doe@company.com. Domain login-secure.example.com is active credential harvester."
}
```

I have added a detailed triage comment to the case.

args.natural_language_query: network connections to IP 203.0.113.42 in the last 24 hours

parts.text:
## TASK

Triage the following Chronicle SecOps case following your instructions.

**Case ID:** CASE-EVAL-002
**Client ID:** eval-client
**Alert Type:** MALWARE
**Initial Severity:** MEDIUM
**Autonomous Mode:** True

Additional context:
Endpoint workstation-07 triggered a malware detection rule. Process powershell.exe launched with encoded command (-EncodedCommand flag). No lateral movement observed yet. User is a developer who occasionally uses PowerShell scripts.

Based on my triage analysis of CASE-EVAL-002:

```json
{
  "case_id": "CASE-EVAL-002",
  "alert_type": "MALWARE",
  "verdict": "SUSPICIOUS",
  "priority": "MEDIUM",
  "confidence_score": 0.65,
  "recommended_action": "MONITOR",
  "key_indicators": ["encoded_powershell", "developer_workstation", "no_lateral_movement"],
  "iocs_found": [{"type": "HOST", "value": "workstation-07"}],
  "summary": "Encoded PowerShell execution detected on workstation-07. No lateral movement or data exfiltration observed. User is a developer — could be legitimate admin activity or early-stage malware.",
  "escalation_notes": "Monitor for additional suspicious activity. Check if PowerShell usage is normal for this user's role."
}
```

args.natural_language_query: process executions on workstation-07 in the last hour

parts.text:
## TASK

Triage the following Chronicle SecOps case following your instructions.

**Case ID:** CASE-EVAL-003
**Client ID:** eval-client
**Alert Type:** SUSPICIOUS_PROCESS
**Initial Severity:** LOW
**Autonomous Mode:** True

Additional context:
Routine vulnerability scanner nessus-scanner.internal triggered a network scan alert. This is a known authorized scanner running weekly security assessments. The source IP 10.0.50.100 is in the scanner subnet 10.0.50.0/24.

Based on my triage analysis of CASE-EVAL-003:

```json
{
  "case_id": "CASE-EVAL-003",
  "alert_type": "SUSPICIOUS_PROCESS",
  "verdict": "BENIGN",
  "priority": "INFORMATIVE",
  "confidence_score": 0.96,
  "recommended_action": "CLOSE_FP",
  "key_indicators": ["known_scanner", "authorized_subnet", "scheduled_scan"],
  "iocs_found": [],
  "summary": "Known authorized vulnerability scanner (Nessus) triggered network scan alert. Source IP in scanner subnet. This is a routine weekly security assessment.",
  "escalation_notes": ""
}
```

## TASK

Triage the following Chronicle SecOps case following your instructions.

**Case ID:** CASE-EVAL-004
**Client ID:** eval-client
**Alert Type:** RANSOMWARE
**Initial Severity:** CRITICAL
**Autonomous Mode:** True

Additional context:
Endpoint finance-server-03 detected rapid file encryption activity. Multiple .locked file extensions observed. Ransom note README_DECRYPT.txt dropped in several directories. Process conhost.exe spawned by unknown parent. Shadow copies deleted via vssadmin.exe. Network shares accessed from this host.

```json
{
  "case_id": "CASE-EVAL-004",
  "alert_type": "RANSOMWARE",
  "verdict": "MALICIOUS",
  "priority": "CRITICAL",
  "confidence_score": 0.97,
  "recommended_action": "CONTAIN",
  "key_indicators": ["file_encryption_activity", "ransom_note_dropped", "shadow_copy_deletion", "network_share_access"],
  "iocs_found": [{"type": "HOST", "value": "finance-server-03"}],
  "summary": "Active ransomware execution on finance-server-03. File encryption in progress, ransom note dropped, shadow copies deleted. Network share access indicates potential lateral spread.",
  "escalation_notes": "IMMEDIATE isolation required. Activate ransomware IRP. Check network shares for spread. Preserve forensic evidence before remediation."
}
```

args.natural_language_query: file modifications and process executions on finance-server-03 in the last hour

parts.text:
## TASK

Triage the following Chronicle SecOps case following your instructions.

**Case ID:** CASE-EVAL-005
**Client ID:** eval-client
**Alert Type:** BRUTE_FORCE
**Initial Severity:** MEDIUM
**Autonomous Mode:** True

Additional context:
527 failed login attempts detected from IP 198.51.100.25 against the VPN gateway in the last 30 minutes. Multiple usernames targeted including admin, svc-backup, and cfo@company.com. Source IP geolocates to a known bulletproof hosting provider.

```json
{
  "case_id": "CASE-EVAL-005",
  "alert_type": "BRUTE_FORCE",
  "verdict": "MALICIOUS",
  "priority": "HIGH",
  "confidence_score": 0.88,
  "recommended_action": "CONTAIN",
  "key_indicators": ["527_failed_logins_30min", "multiple_usernames_targeted", "bulletproof_hosting_source", "vpn_gateway_targeted"],
  "iocs_found": [{"type": "IP", "value": "198.51.100.25"}],
  "summary": "Active brute force attack against VPN gateway. 527 failed attempts from bulletproof hosting IP. Multiple high-value accounts targeted including admin and C-level.",
  "escalation_notes": "Block source IP at perimeter. Check if any targeted accounts were successfully compromised. Review VPN logs for successful authentications from this IP."
}
```

args.natural_language_query: failed login attempts from IP 198.51.100.25 in the last hour

parts.text:
## TASK

Triage the following Chronicle SecOps case following your instructions.

**Case ID:** CASE-EVAL-006
**Client ID:** eval-client
**Alert Type:** LATERAL_MOVEMENT
**Initial Severity:** HIGH
**Autonomous Mode:** True

Additional context:
Service account svc-deploy initiated RDP sessions to 12 different hosts within 15 minutes. This account normally only connects to 2 deployment servers. Destinations include domain controllers DC-01 and DC-02, file server FS-CORP, and multiple workstations. Source is jump server JUMP-01.

```json
{
  "case_id": "CASE-EVAL-006",
  "alert_type": "LATERAL_MOVEMENT",
  "verdict": "MALICIOUS",
  "priority": "HIGH",
  "confidence_score": 0.88,
  "recommended_action": "CONTAIN",
  "key_indicators": ["anomalous_rdp_pattern", "service_account_abuse", "domain_controller_access", "12_hosts_15_minutes"],
  "iocs_found": [{"type": "USER", "value": "svc-deploy"}, {"type": "HOST", "value": "JUMP-01"}, {"type": "HOST", "value": "DC-01"}, {"type": "HOST", "value": "DC-02"}],
  "summary": "Service account svc-deploy showing anomalous lateral movement via RDP. 12 hosts accessed in 15 minutes including domain controllers. Normal baseline is 2 servers. Indicates possible credential compromise.",
  "escalation_notes": "Isolate JUMP-01. Disable svc-deploy account. Check for Golden Ticket / Pass-the-Hash. Audit all accessed hosts for persistence."
}
```

args.natural_language_query: RDP connections from service account svc-deploy in the last hour

parts.text:
## TASK

Triage the following Chronicle SecOps case following your instructions.

**Case ID:** CASE-EVAL-007
**Client ID:** eval-client
**Alert Type:** DATA_EXFILTRATION
**Initial Severity:** HIGH
**Autonomous Mode:** True

Additional context:
DLP alert triggered: 2.3 GB uploaded to mega.nz from workstation HR-WS-04 at 2:47 AM local time. User account is hr.analyst@company.com. Files include salary_data_2026.xlsx, employee_ssn_backup.csv, and performance_reviews_q1.zip. The upload occurred outside business hours.

```json
{
  "case_id": "CASE-EVAL-007",
  "alert_type": "DATA_EXFILTRATION",
  "verdict": "SUSPICIOUS",
  "priority": "HIGH",
  "confidence_score": 0.78,
  "recommended_action": "ESCALATE_T2",
  "key_indicators": ["large_upload_2.3GB", "sensitive_PII_data", "cloud_storage_mega.nz", "after_hours_activity", "hr_sensitive_files"],
  "iocs_found": [{"type": "DOMAIN", "value": "mega.nz"}, {"type": "USER", "value": "hr.analyst@company.com"}, {"type": "HOST", "value": "HR-WS-04"}],
  "summary": "2.3 GB of sensitive HR data (SSNs, salary, reviews) uploaded to mega.nz at 2:47 AM from HR workstation. Could be insider threat or compromised account. PII exposure risk.",
  "escalation_notes": "Requires T2 investigation: verify with user if upload was authorized. Coordinate with HR/Legal for insider threat assessment. Check account for compromise indicators."
}
```

args.natural_language_query: network uploads from HR-WS-04 to external destinations in the last 24 hours

parts.text:
## TASK

Triage the following Chronicle SecOps case following your instructions.

**Case ID:** CASE-EVAL-008
**Client ID:** eval-client
**Alert Type:** DNS_ANOMALY
**Initial Severity:** MEDIUM
**Autonomous Mode:** True

Additional context:
Endpoint DEV-WS-12 generating high volume of DNS TXT queries to subdomains of xk7f9.duckdns.org. Average query length is 180+ characters with high entropy in subdomain labels. 4,200 queries in the last hour. The domain was registered 3 days ago.

```json
{
  "case_id": "CASE-EVAL-008",
  "alert_type": "DNS_ANOMALY",
  "verdict": "SUSPICIOUS",
  "priority": "MEDIUM",
  "confidence_score": 0.72,
  "recommended_action": "ESCALATE_T2",
  "key_indicators": ["high_entropy_dns_queries", "txt_record_abuse", "duckdns_dynamic_domain", "4200_queries_per_hour", "newly_registered_domain"],
  "iocs_found": [{"type": "DOMAIN", "value": "xk7f9.duckdns.org"}, {"type": "HOST", "value": "DEV-WS-12"}],
  "summary": "Possible DNS tunneling from DEV-WS-12. High-entropy TXT queries to newly registered DuckDNS domain. 4,200 queries/hour with 180+ char subdomain labels consistent with DNS exfiltration or C2.",
  "escalation_notes": "Investigate DEV-WS-12 for malware. Block xk7f9.duckdns.org at DNS. Analyze query payloads for data exfiltration patterns."
}
```

args.natural_language_query: DNS queries from DEV-WS-12 to duckdns.org in the last 2 hours

parts.text:
## TASK

Triage the following Chronicle SecOps case following your instructions.

**Case ID:** CASE-EVAL-009
**Client ID:** eval-client
**Alert Type:** CRYPTOMINING
**Initial Severity:** MEDIUM
**Autonomous Mode:** True

Additional context:
Process xmrig.exe detected on cloud VM web-server-prod-02. CPU utilization spiked to 98%. Outbound connection to stratum+tcp://pool.minexmr.com:4444. The process was spawned by a cron job added 2 hours ago under the www-data user.

```json
{
  "case_id": "CASE-EVAL-009",
  "alert_type": "CRYPTOMINING",
  "verdict": "MALICIOUS",
  "priority": "MEDIUM",
  "confidence_score": 0.93,
  "recommended_action": "CONTAIN",
  "key_indicators": ["xmrig_binary", "mining_pool_connection", "98_percent_cpu", "unauthorized_cron_job", "production_server"],
  "iocs_found": [{"type": "HOST", "value": "web-server-prod-02"}, {"type": "DOMAIN", "value": "pool.minexmr.com"}],
  "summary": "Confirmed cryptomining on production web server. xmrig.exe connecting to mining pool via unauthorized cron job under www-data. Indicates server compromise — likely via web application vulnerability.",
  "escalation_notes": "Kill mining process. Remove unauthorized cron. Investigate web-server-prod-02 for initial access vector. Check for additional persistence."
}
```

args.natural_language_query: process launches and network connections from web-server-prod-02 in the last 4 hours

parts.text:
## TASK

Triage the following Chronicle SecOps case following your instructions.

**Case ID:** CASE-EVAL-010
**Client ID:** eval-client
**Alert Type:** C2_COMMUNICATION
**Initial Severity:** HIGH
**Autonomous Mode:** True

Additional context:
Endpoint SALES-WS-09 making periodic HTTPS connections to 185.220.101.42 every 60±5 seconds on port 8443. Beacon pattern detected over 6 hours. The destination IP has no reverse DNS and is hosted at a VPS provider in Eastern Europe. Small data transfer per connection (2-5 KB).

```json
{
  "case_id": "CASE-EVAL-010",
  "alert_type": "C2_COMMUNICATION",
  "verdict": "MALICIOUS",
  "priority": "HIGH",
  "confidence_score": 0.90,
  "recommended_action": "CONTAIN",
  "key_indicators": ["periodic_beacon_60s", "non_standard_port_8443", "no_reverse_dns", "eastern_europe_vps", "6_hour_duration"],
  "iocs_found": [{"type": "IP", "value": "185.220.101.42"}, {"type": "HOST", "value": "SALES-WS-09"}],
  "summary": "C2 beacon detected. SALES-WS-09 beaconing to Eastern European VPS every 60 seconds on port 8443 for 6 hours. Pattern, timing regularity, and small payload size are classic C2 indicators.",
  "escalation_notes": "Isolate SALES-WS-09 immediately. Block IP 185.220.101.42. Collect memory dump before remediation. Check for data staging or exfiltration."
}
```

args.natural_language_query: network connections from SALES-WS-09 to IP 185.220.101.42

parts.text:
## TASK

Triage the following Chronicle SecOps case following your instructions.

**Case ID:** CASE-EVAL-011
**Client ID:** eval-client
**Alert Type:** IMPOSSIBLE_TRAVEL
**Initial Severity:** MEDIUM
**Autonomous Mode:** True

Additional context:
User cfo@company.com authenticated to Microsoft 365 from Chicago, USA at 14:23 UTC and from Lagos, Nigeria at 14:31 UTC — 8 minutes apart. The Chicago session used a known corporate device. The Lagos session used an unregistered device with a different user-agent string (Linux/Chrome vs Windows/Edge).

```json
{
  "case_id": "CASE-EVAL-011",
  "alert_type": "IMPOSSIBLE_TRAVEL",
  "verdict": "SUSPICIOUS",
  "priority": "HIGH",
  "confidence_score": 0.82,
  "recommended_action": "ESCALATE_T2",
  "key_indicators": ["impossible_travel_8_minutes", "different_device", "different_user_agent", "high_value_account_cfo", "unregistered_device"],
  "iocs_found": [{"type": "USER", "value": "cfo@company.com"}, {"type": "IP", "value": "Lagos source IP"}],
  "summary": "Impossible travel detected for CFO account. Chicago to Lagos in 8 minutes with different devices and user-agents. High probability of credential compromise for C-level account.",
  "escalation_notes": "Verify with CFO if VPN or travel is involved. Different device + user-agent strongly suggests compromise. Consider precautionary session revocation."
}
```

args.natural_language_query: login events for user cfo@company.com in the last 24 hours

parts.text:
## TASK

Triage the following Chronicle SecOps case following your instructions.

**Case ID:** CASE-EVAL-012
**Client ID:** eval-client
**Alert Type:** PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION
**Initial Severity:** HIGH
**Autonomous Mode:** True

Additional context:
User intern.jones@company.com was added to the Domain Admins group at 03:15 UTC. The change was made from workstation INTERN-WS-01. No change request ticket exists in ServiceNow. The user's normal group memberships are: Interns, ReadOnly-FileShare. The modification was performed using net group command via cmd.exe.

```json
{
  "case_id": "CASE-EVAL-012",
  "alert_type": "PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION",
  "verdict": "SUSPICIOUS",
  "priority": "HIGH",
  "confidence_score": 0.85,
  "recommended_action": "ESCALATE_T2",
  "key_indicators": ["domain_admins_addition", "intern_account", "no_change_ticket", "after_hours_03:15", "cmd_net_group"],
  "iocs_found": [{"type": "USER", "value": "intern.jones@company.com"}, {"type": "HOST", "value": "INTERN-WS-01"}],
  "summary": "Unauthorized privilege escalation. Intern account added to Domain Admins at 3:15 AM via command line with no change ticket. Strong indicator of compromised credentials or insider threat.",
  "escalation_notes": "Remove from Domain Admins immediately. Disable account pending investigation. Check INTERN-WS-01 for malware. Audit all actions taken with elevated privileges."
}
```

## TASK

Triage the following Chronicle SecOps case following your instructions.

**Case ID:** CASE-EVAL-013
**Client ID:** eval-client
**Alert Type:** PERSISTENCE
**Initial Severity:** MEDIUM
**Autonomous Mode:** True

Additional context:
New scheduled task 'WindowsUpdateCheck' created on endpoint ACCT-WS-03 via schtasks.exe. Task runs every 15 minutes and executes a PowerShell script from C:\ProgramData\tmp\update.ps1. The script was not present 24 hours ago. Task was created by the local SYSTEM account.

```json
{
  "case_id": "CASE-EVAL-013",
  "alert_type": "PERSISTENCE",
  "verdict": "SUSPICIOUS",
  "priority": "MEDIUM",
  "confidence_score": 0.70,
  "recommended_action": "ESCALATE_T2",
  "key_indicators": ["new_scheduled_task", "powershell_payload", "programdata_tmp_path", "15_minute_interval", "system_account_creation"],
  "iocs_found": [{"type": "HOST", "value": "ACCT-WS-03"}],
  "summary": "Suspicious persistence mechanism. New scheduled task running PowerShell from temp directory every 15 minutes. Script appeared in last 24 hours. Mimics Windows Update naming to evade detection.",
  "escalation_notes": "Analyze update.ps1 content. Check ACCT-WS-03 for initial access indicators. Review how SYSTEM created this task — likely exploit or malware."
}
```

args.natural_language_query: scheduled task creation and PowerShell executions on ACCT-WS-03

parts.text:
## TASK

Triage the following Chronicle SecOps case following your instructions.

**Case ID:** CASE-EVAL-014
**Client ID:** eval-client
**Alert Type:** INSIDER_THREAT
**Initial Severity:** MEDIUM
**Autonomous Mode:** True

Additional context:
User departing.employee@company.com accessed 847 files on the engineering file share in the last 2 hours. Normal daily access for this user is ~30 files. The user submitted their resignation 5 days ago. Files include source code repositories, architecture documents, and client API keys. No file downloads detected yet but clipboard activity is elevated.

```json
{
  "case_id": "CASE-EVAL-014",
  "alert_type": "INSIDER_THREAT",
  "verdict": "SUSPICIOUS",
  "priority": "HIGH",
  "confidence_score": 0.75,
  "recommended_action": "ESCALATE_T2",
  "key_indicators": ["847_files_vs_30_baseline", "departing_employee", "source_code_access", "api_keys_access", "elevated_clipboard_activity"],
  "iocs_found": [{"type": "USER", "value": "departing.employee@company.com"}],
  "summary": "Departing employee accessed 28x their normal file volume including source code and API keys. Resignation submitted 5 days ago. Elevated clipboard activity suggests preparation for data theft. No confirmed exfiltration yet.",
  "escalation_notes": "Coordinate with HR and Legal. Monitor for download/upload activity. Consider restricting file share access. Rotate accessed API keys as precaution."
}
```

args.natural_language_query: file access events by departing.employee@company.com in the last 24 hours

parts.text:
## TASK

Triage the following Chronicle SecOps case following your instructions.

**Case ID:** CASE-EVAL-015
**Client ID:** eval-client
**Alert Type:** SUSPICIOUS_OUTBOUND
**Initial Severity:** LOW
**Autonomous Mode:** True

Additional context:
Multiple workstations generating high-volume HTTPS traffic to download.windowsupdate.com and delivery.mp.microsoft.com. Traffic spike correlates with Patch Tuesday (second Tuesday of month). All source endpoints are domain-joined corporate workstations running WSUS-managed updates.

```json
{
  "case_id": "CASE-EVAL-015",
  "alert_type": "SUSPICIOUS_OUTBOUND",
  "verdict": "BENIGN",
  "priority": "INFORMATIVE",
  "confidence_score": 0.98,
  "recommended_action": "CLOSE_FP",
  "key_indicators": ["microsoft_update_domains", "patch_tuesday_correlation", "wsus_managed", "domain_joined_endpoints"],
  "iocs_found": [],
  "summary": "Patch Tuesday Windows Update traffic. All traffic to legitimate Microsoft update infrastructure from WSUS-managed corporate endpoints. Expected behavior.",
  "escalation_notes": ""
}
```

args.comment: Triage assessment — benign Windows Update traffic
